_comment: Sample registry.json (v3 schema) demonstrating the on-disk format the local dev harness serves at /registry. The local_registry_server.js loads this file and serves it; mtime-based hot-reload means editing the file is picked up on the next request without restarting OSD. Replace the example plugins below with your real plugin entries when integrating with a real build. See packages/osd-mfe/src/registry/registry_provider.ts for the parsing logic and packages/osd-mfe/src/registry/v3_schema.proposal.md for the schema reference.

default._comment: The 'default' dimension is the catch-all routing slot. Every consumer falls back to this when no per-customer or per-bucket override matches. See packages/osd-mfe/src/registry/resolve.ts for the resolution order.

example_hello._comment: Minimal example: a 'hello world' plugin. Loaded eagerly on boot. integrity is sha384 of the UNCOMPRESSED remoteEntry.js bytes — see packages/osd-mfe/src/bootstrap/load_remote.ts.

example_lazy._comment: Example showing lazy-loaded chunks. The remoteEntry.js declares an MF container that webpack will fetch lazy chunks for on demand (e.g., when navigating to a route this plugin owns).

example_external_cdn._comment: Example showing a plugin hosted on an external CDN instead of the local :8080 origin. For this URL to load: (a) the host must allow-list the origin via opensearchDashboards.mfe.cdnOrigin or opensearchDashboards.mfe.devOverrideOrigins in the served CSP; (b) the external CDN must serve cross-origin with appropriate CORS headers (so the browser can verify SRI).
